message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Adding opt-in debug/trace logging to StreamListener (guarded DEBUG logs at start/end/flush/reset detection). Only touching this one file. Let me know your feature's files so we can confirm no overlap.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/messages.py
note: I'm implementing idle_timeout_s/tick() heartbeat in streaming_listener.py only. I saw is_last_chunk is a separate feature (likely yours) touching messages.py/StreamResponse - I'll integrate with it defensively (setattr/hasattr) without editing messages.py myself. Let me know if you need me to touch anything else.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streamify.py
note: Conflict: my feature (idle_timeout_s + tick()) requires structural changes to StreamListener - new __init__ params, new self._last_chunk_ts state, edits inside receive() to refresh the timestamp, and a new tick() method. That's most of the file's surface area, including the exact start/end/flush/reset-detection logic you mentioned wanting to instrument. Layering independent logging edits into the same regions concurrently is high risk for clobbering. Proposal: I take streaming_listener.py entirely for this PR. If your debug/trace logging is meant to observe listener lifecycle events, could it live in dspy/streaming/streamify.py (which drives the listeners) instead, or as a follow-up patch applied after mine lands? Open to other splits if this doesn't work for your feature.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Same file conflict: my feature is opt-in debug/trace logging (debug flag + guarded self._logger.debug(...) calls at start-detection, rolling-end-check, emit-chunk, flush, reset in receive()/flush()), purely additive, no control-flow changes. Since our features are orthogonal (logging vs idle-timeout/tick), let's both edit streaming_listener.py but keep diffs scoped to our own additions only: I will not touch tick()/idle_timeout_s logic, please don't touch my debug/_logger lines. I'll implement now and send DONE with a summary of exact lines changed so you can rebase/integrate cleanly.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Concern isn't logical overlap, it's that we'd both be running independent Read/Edit tool calls against the same live file at the same time - that can literally clobber each other's writes (stale-read-then-overwrite), not just merge-conflict. Proposal to make this safe without giving up the shared file: let's sequence instead of parallelize. My change is small (init params + a few lines in receive() + one new tick() method) - I'll implement now and send DONE the moment it's finished. Please hold off editing streaming_listener.py until you see my DONE, then layer your debug/_logger additions on top of my version. That gives us both the file with zero risk of lost writes. Sound OK? If you'd rather go first, say so and I'll wait for your DONE instead.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Can't relocate to streamify.py - my required log points (start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk, flush, reset) need direct access to StreamListener internals (field_start_queue, field_end_queue, adapter_identifiers, buffer contents) that streamify.py doesn't have; task spec also explicitly names streaming_listener.py as the only file to modify. Good news: our two features are purely additive and don't touch the same logic - I only add new __init__ kwargs (debug, debug_logger) + guarded self._logger.debug(...) calls interleaved at existing decision points (no control-flow changes, no line deletions), you add idle_timeout_s/tick()/last_chunk_ts. Since neither of us restructures existing lines, this should merge as a straightforward union. I'll proceed now, keep every change additive (no edits to lines you're touching), and send DONE with the exact diff so you can reconcile easily. OK to proceed in parallel?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed - sequencing works. You go first, I'll wait for your DONE before touching streaming_listener.py, then layer my debug/_logger additions on top of your version additively.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: OK, proceeding in parallel on streaming_listener.py. I'll only touch: __init__ signature (adding idle_timeout_s param + self._last_chunk_ts), a small addition inside receive() to refresh self._last_chunk_ts on non-empty chunks, and a new tick() method. I won't touch debug/_logger lines or your __init__ kwargs. Will send DONE when finished.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Just confirming final approach given crossed messages: sequential, not parallel - you go first (init params, last_chunk_ts, tick(), receive() timestamp refresh), send DONE, then I layer my debug/_logger additions on top. I will not edit the file until I see your DONE.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished. Added: idle_timeout_s param + self._last_chunk_ts state in __init__; receive() now sets self._last_chunk_ts = time.time() whenever chunk_message is non-empty; new tick(now_ts=None) method that, when idle_timeout_s is set and stream_start and not stream_end and idle_timeout_s has elapsed since _last_chunk_ts, emits StreamResponse(predict_name, signature_field_name, chunk='') (with is_last_chunk=True auto-included only if that field exists on StreamResponse, so it's forward-compatible with your/feature1's addition) and unconditionally resets stream_start/stream_end/cache_hit/field_start_queue/field_end_queue/_last_chunk_ts regardless of allow_reuse. All existing tests in tests/streaming pass. File is all yours now to layer debug/_logger additions on top.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished. Added debug/debug_logger params to __init__, self._logger, module-level _debug_preview() truncation helper, and guarded self._logger.debug(...) calls (isEnabledFor(DEBUG) gated) at: reset (allow_reuse block), start detection (both the cache-hit path and the normal field_start_queue path), rolling end check (regex match in stream_start block), emit chunk (before returning StreamResponse), and flush() (before return, refactored the three branch returns into a single 'result' var + one log + one return). No control-flow changes; all existing tests in tests/streaming pass; ruff clean. Wrote patch.txt via git diff. Should be a clean additive layer on top of your idle_timeout_s/tick() work if patches are combined.
